thm:3.10.summary: Perfectoid rings $=$ perfect prisms. Equivalence of categories between perfectoid rings $R$ (in the BMS1 sense) and perfect prisms $(A,I)$, via $R \mapsto (A_{\mathrm{inf}}(R), \ker \theta)$ and $(A,I) \mapsto A/I$. The structural theorem that turns the perfectoid framework into a deformation problem on $\delta$-rings.

thm:1.8.summary: Master theorem: for a bounded prism $(A,I)$ and smooth $p$-adic formal scheme $X/A/I$, prismatic cohomology $R\Gamma_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(X/A)$ specializes to crystalline (when $I = (p)$, thm:5.2), Hodge-Tate (thm:6.3), de Rham (thm:6.4), and étale (thm:9.1) comparisons. The theorem the rest of the paper assembles.

thm:4.11.summary: Hodge-Tate comparison (formal): the map $\eta^*_X: \Omega^*_{X/(A/I)} \to H^*(\overline{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_{X/A})\{*\}$ is an isomorphism of dg $A/I$-algebras. The graded form of $H^i \cong \Omega^i\{-i\}$ is the technical heart making prismatic cohomology computable.

thm:5.2.summary: Crystalline comparison in characteristic $p$: $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{R^{(1)}/A} \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}(R/A)$ canonically, compatibly with Frobenius. Recovers crystalline cohomology as a special case of prismatic; the simplest nontrivial verification of thm:1.8.

thm:6.3.summary: Hodge-Tate comparison (mixed characteristic): $\Omega^i_{X/(A/I)} \to H^i(\overline{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_{X/A})\{i\}$ is an isomorphism of étale sheaves. Promotes thm:4.11 to mixed characteristic — the geometric content of prismatic cohomology.

thm:6.4.summary: De Rham comparison: $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{X/A} \widehat{\otimes}^L_{A,\phi} A/I \simeq \Omega^*_{X/(A/I)}$. The tensor product along Frobenius recovers de Rham; provides another specialization in the master theorem thm:1.8.

thm:1.12.summary: Semiperfectoid rings: any $p$-adically complete quotient $S$ of a perfectoid ring admits a universal prism $(\mathbbl{\Delta}_S^{\mathrm{init}}, I)$ with $I = (d)$ principal, and a perfectoidization $S \to S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$. The structural extension of perfectoid theory beyond the perfectoid case.

thm:7.4.summary: The perfectoidization map $S \to S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ is surjective. The technical fact that lets perfectoidization behave like a quotient construction.

thm:7.14.summary: André's flatness lemma: every perfectoid ring $R$ admits a $p$-completely faithfully flat map to a perfectoid $S$ that is absolutely integrally closed (every monic polynomial has a root). The strong flatness input to the almost-purity proof.

thm:1.14.summary: Quasiregular semiperfectoid case: when $S$ is quasiregular semiperfectoid (bounded $p^\infty$-torsion + $p$-completely flat shifted cotangent complex), the prismatic cohomology $\mathbbl{\Delta}_S$ is discrete, $(p,I)$-completely flat, and the universal prism in the prismatic site of $S$.

thm:9.1.summary: Étale comparison: $R\mu_* \mathbf{Z}/p^n \simeq (\mathbbl{\Delta}_{X/A}[1/d]/p^n)^{\phi=1}$ via nearby cycles to the adic generic fibre. Recovers étale cohomology as Frobenius fixed points of inverted-$d$ prismatic cohomology — the étale specialization in thm:1.8.

thm:9.4.summary: For $R$ perfectoid: $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)(R) \simeq R\Gamma(\mathrm{Spec}(R[1/p]), \mathbf{Z}_p(n))$ for $n \geq 1$. The arithmetic content of étale comparison restricted to the perfectoid case.

thm:1.17.summary: Almost purity (intro statement): for $R$ perfectoid and $S$ an integral $R$-algebra finite étale away from $V(J)$ (finitely generated $J \subset R$), $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ is a perfectoid ring (thm:10.11) and $R/p^n \to S_{\mathrm{perfd}}/p^n$ is almost finite étale (thm:10.9). The Faltings-style almost-purity theorem in the prismatic framework.

thm:10.9.summary: Almost purity (technical form): for $R$ perfectoid, $J \subset R$ finitely generated, $S$ finitely presented finite over $R$ with $\mathrm{Spec}(S) \to \mathrm{Spec}(R)$ finite étale outside $V(J)$, the perfectoidization $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ is discrete perfectoid, isomorphic to $S$ away from $V(J)$, and $R/p^n \to S_{\mathrm{perfd}}/p^n$ is almost finite étale.

thm:10.11.summary: For $R$ perfectoid corresponding to perfect prism $(A,I)$ and $R \to S$ the $p$-completion of an integral map, $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{S/A,\mathrm{perf}}$ is discrete and a perfect $p$-complete $\delta$-$A$-algebra; consequently $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ is discrete perfectoid. The integral perfectoidization construction underwriting almost purity.

thm:11.1.summary: $\mathbf{F}_p$-étale cohomological dimension: for $R$ perfectoid, $\mathrm{Spec}(R[1/p])$ has $\mathbf{F}_p$-étale cohomological dimension $\le 1$. Counterintuitively low — a structural application of almost purity.

thm:1.16.summary: Nygaard filtration on the prismatic site: for a bounded prism $(A,I)$ and smooth affine $X = \mathrm{Spf}(R)/A/I$, prismatic cohomology equips a quasisyntomic-site sheaf with a Nygaard filtration on its Frobenius twist. The $p$-adic Hodge / motivic-filtration content.

thm:12.2.summary: Nygaard graded $\cong$ Hodge-Tate filtered: $\mathrm{gr}^i_N \mathbbl{\Delta}_S \cong \mathrm{Fil}_i \overline{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_S \{i\}$ via $\phi/d^i$. Identifies graded pieces of the Nygaard filtration with Frobenius-twisted Hodge-Tate filtration pieces.

thm:13.1.summary: Nygaard completion is $\delta$-ring: $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_S$ admits a functorial $\delta$-ring structure refining $\phi$, identifying it with the Nygaard completion of $\mathbbl{\Delta}_S$ compatibly with filtrations. Bridge to BMS2.

thm:14.1.summary: $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ sheaves are discrete and $p$-torsion-free for all $n \geq 0$. The structural input for the odd $K$-theory vanishing in thm:1.18.

thm:1.18.summary: Quasisyntomic-local odd vanishing: BMS2's $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ are concentrated in degree $0$ and $p$-torsion-free; locally in the quasisyntomic topology, mod-$p$ algebraic $K$-theory $K(-)/p$ is concentrated in even degrees. Application to algebraic $K$-theory.

thm:15.3.summary: Quasisyntomic resolution: $R\Gamma_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(X/A) \cong R\Gamma(X_{\mathrm{qsyn}}, \mathbbl{\Delta}_{-/A})$ canonically, compatibly with Nygaard filtrations. Lets prismatic cohomology be computed via quasisyntomic descent — proves the relative Nygaard structure of thm:1.16.

thm:15.2.summary: For large quasisyntomic $S$, derived prismatic cohomology $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{S/A}$ is concentrated in degree $0$, $(p,I)$-completely flat, and the initial object of the relative prismatic site. Gives the local model for thm:15.3.

thm:16.14.summary: $q$-crystalline / crystalline comparison: $q\Omega_{R/D} \widehat{\otimes}^L_D D/(q-1) \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}(R/(D/(q-1)))$. Specializes the $q$-deformation back to ordinary crystalline cohomology.

thm:16.18.summary: $q$-crystalline / prismatic comparison: for $p$-completely smooth $R/D/I$, $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{R^{(1)}/D} \simeq q\Omega_{R/D}$ canonically. Identifies the prismatic cohomology of a Frobenius twist with $q$-de Rham cohomology.

thm:17.2.summary: Comparison with $A\Omega$ (BMS1): $A\Omega_R \simeq q\Omega_{R/A} \simeq \mathbbl{\Delta}_{R^{(1)}/A} = \phi_A^* \mathbbl{\Delta}_{R/A}$ as $E_\infty$-$A$-algebras compatible with Frobenius. Recovers BMS1's $A\Omega$ as Frobenius-twisted prismatic cohomology — the integration of BMS1 into the prismatic framework.

edges.note: Crystalline-comparison case of the master theorem.
Hodge-Tate-comparison case of the master theorem.
De Rham comparison case.
Étale comparison case.
Mixed-characteristic Hodge-Tate uses the formal/algebraic Hodge-Tate isomorphism as input.
De Rham comparison reduces along Frobenius to Hodge-Tate.
Char-$p$ case is computed by the perfect-prism dictionary specialized to the Frobenius twist.
Coefficient ring $A/I$ comes from a (possibly non-perfect) prism whose perfect base case relies on this dictionary.
Surjectivity of perfectoidization is part of the semiperfectoid structural package.
Universal prism for a semiperfectoid sits over the perfect-prism dictionary on its perfectoid quotient.
Quasiregular semiperfectoid case sharpens the general semiperfectoid structure.
Discreteness and flatness on quasiregular semiperfectoids is the input the qsyn comparison uses globally.
Almost purity intro statement is the body theorem thm:10.9.
Perfectoidization of integral algebras is the integral-extension half of the intro statement.
Almost étale follows once $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ is constructed and shown discrete perfectoid.
André's flatness lemma is the key flatness input that makes the almost-étale conclusion go through after pulling back to absolutely integrally closed cover.
Surjectivity of perfectoidization is used to prove discreteness of $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ in the integral case.
Perfect-prism dictionary identifies $S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$ with its $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$ side.
André's lemma constructs the cover at the level of perfectoid rings, which corresponds to a perfect-prism cover via the dictionary.
Étale comparison routes through Hodge-Tate and inverted-$d$ prismatic to identify nearby-cycles cohomology.
Statement is over a perfectoid base, identified via the perfect-prism dictionary.
Perfectoid case of étale comparison.
Cohomological-dimension bound uses almost purity to pass between $R$ and integral covers.
Discreteness of $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ on perfectoids is the key local computation.
Quasiregular semiperfectoid descent computes $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ globally.
Odd-$K$-theory vanishing is the consequence of $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ discreteness via the BMS2 motivic filtration.
Existence + sheafiness of the Nygaard filtration is established via quasisyntomic descent.
Global qsyn statement is glued from the local large-quasisyntomic case.
Quasiregular semiperfectoid descent gives the local model used to define the qsyn sheaf.
Graded comparison runs inside the Nygaard-filtered qsyn sheaf.
Nygaard completion lives on the qsyn-sheaf object built in thm:1.16.
$q$-crystalline specialization at $q=1$ recovers ordinary crystalline; consistency check against thm:5.2.
Identifies $q$-de Rham cohomology with prismatic cohomology of the Frobenius twist over the $q$-prism $(D, [p]_q)$.
$A\Omega$-comparison is the $q$-crystalline / prismatic identification specialized to the perfectoid base.
De Rham comparison is consistent with the BMS1 specialization.

prism_foundations.description: Sections 2–3. $\delta$-rings, prisms, the perfect-prism / perfectoid dictionary. The structural foundation: prisms are the deformation-theoretic carriers of perfectoid information.

prismatic_cohomology_and_comparisons.description: Sections 4–6. Prismatic cohomology $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{X/A}$, Hodge-Tate comparison, de Rham comparison, char-$p$ crystalline comparison. The unification of $p$-adic cohomology theories the paper achieves.

perfectoidization.description: Sections 7, 10. Semiperfectoid rings, perfectoidization $S \mapsto S_{\mathrm{perfd}}$, André's flatness lemma. The category-theoretic bridge from non-perfectoid quotients back to the perfectoid framework.

almost_purity_and_etale.description: Sections 9–11. Étale comparison, almost purity for perfectoid rings, $\mathbf{F}_p$-étale cohomological dimension. The arithmetic-geometric content (and the part Lean formalizers care most about).

nygaard_and_K_theory.description: Sections 12–15. Nygaard filtration, quasisyntomic descent, $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$ discreteness, application to algebraic $K$-theory.

q_crystalline_and_BMS_bridge.description: Sections 16–17. $q$-crystalline / $q$-de Rham cohomology, comparison with $A\Omega$ (BMS1). Integrates the prior BMS work as Frobenius-twisted prismatic.

Scholze:Perfectoid.title: Perfectoid spaces
Scholze:Perfectoid.role: Defines perfectoid rings; perfect-prism dictionary thm:3.10 makes them equivalent to perfect prisms.

BMS1.title: Integral p-adic Hodge theory
BMS1.role: Predecessor; $A\Omega$ recovered as Frobenius-twisted prismatic in thm:17.2.

BMS2.title: Topological Hochschild homology and integral p-adic Hodge theory
BMS2.role: Source of the quasisyntomic / motivic filtration; thm:1.18 + thm:14.1 are about the BMS2 sheaves $\mathbf{Z}_p(n)$.

Andre:Perfectoid.title: Le lemme d'Abhyankar perfectoide
Andre:Perfectoid.role: Source of André's flatness lemma; thm:7.14 imports / sharpens it.

FaltingsAlmostEtale.title: Almost étale extensions
FaltingsAlmostEtale.role: Original almost-purity setting; thm:1.17 / thm:10.9 is the prismatic-framework version.

GabberRamero.title: Almost ring theory
GabberRamero.role: Almost mathematics foundations used in the almost-purity proof.

Fontaine:CDVR.title: Le corps des périodes p-adiques
Fontaine:CDVR.role: $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$ construction; perfect-prism dictionary thm:3.10 is via $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$.
